package handler

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"sync"
)

// Positive scenario for background_worker_started_from_http_handler_registration.
// Rule intent: Route or middleware setup that also starts unrelated background jobs.
type BackgroundWorkerModel struct {
	ID     string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
	Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
}

var workerOnce sync.Once

func RegisterRoutes(r *gin.Engine, db *gorm.DB) {
	workerOnce.Do(func() {
		go func() {
			for {
				var pending []BackgroundWorkerModel
				db.Where("status = ?", "pending").Find(&pending)
				for _, r := range pending {
					log.Printf("processing worker item %s", r.ID)
				}
			}
		}()
	})

	r.GET("/orders", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var orders []BackgroundWorkerModel
		db.Find(&orders)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"orders": orders})
	})

	r.POST("/orders", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var order BackgroundWorkerModel
		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&order); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		db.Create(&order)
		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"id": order.ID})
	})
}
